assessor_1_raw:
## Assessment 1: ATO Detection

Account-level random holdout = temporal leakage (ATO attack patterns evolve). Average precision = threshold-agnostic metric misaligned with 0.2% FPR operational constraint. Verdict: critique_wins
assessor_2_raw:
## Assessment 2: ATO Detection

Two concrete issues: random holdout temporal leakage; average precision vs. fixed-FPR operational metric. Verdict: critique_wins
assessor_3_raw:
## Assessment 3: ATO Detection

Account-level random split allows future authentication events into training. Average precision integrates over threshold-irrelevant FPR range. Both documented flaws. Verdict: critique_wins
adjudication_raw: Ensemble Synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins. Verdict: critique_wins
